title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Inside Trump’s ‘Royal Court’
items.description: The Atlantic staff writers Ashley Parker and Michael Scherer explain how Trump’s White House works.

items.title: When Bitcoin prices turned against Michael Saylor, he quietly pivoted to risky financial gambit at Strategy
items.description: Saylor has been offseting the Bitcoin drag by reverting to different and dangerous scheme: Issuing tons of preferred stock.

items.title: Was Anyone at Fault in Sierra Nevada Avalanche Deaths?

items.title: Cuba is quickly nearing a point of no return as the U.S. weaponizes its Venezuelan oil supplies
items.description: A U.S. resolution in Cuba may not require full regime change, as the Cuban leadership rapidly runs out of options.

items.title: Mixed Signals Churn Stock Market Despite Recent Highs
items.description: Major indexes have mostly been treading water for months, as investors’ exuberance for artificial intelligence wears off.

items.title: As Gates’ Epstein connection unnerves staff, his foundation looks for a way forward
items.description: The billionaire’s abrupt withdrawal from India’s AI Summit follows renewed scrutiny over his relations with Jeffrey Epstein.

items.title: Troubled Nvidia ally Supermicro pledged to hire a new CFO ‘immediately.’ That was 14 months ago.
items.description: “No one wants this job,” Cowen Partners search firm president Shawn Cole said.

items.title: Another Winter Storm Could Hit the Northeast This Weekend
items.description: There’s a lot of hype, but not a lot of certainty among forecasters yet. Here are three scenarios to watch.

items.title: Austrian Man Found Guilty in Girlfriend’s Death on Mountain Hike
items.description: Thomas Plamberger was given a suspended prison sentence and fined in a case that tested the limits of personal responsibility in mountaineering.

items.title: A Makeup Ban in Syria for Women in Government and Public Sector Stirs Outrage
items.description: A provincial governor wants to prohibit women in government and public sector jobs from wearing makeup, stirring outrage and many, many memes.

items.title: A New U.S. Blockade Is Strangling Cuba
items.description: An analysis of ship movements shows that the Trump administration is isolating the island at one of its most vulnerable moments.

items.title: Former Prince Andrew was arrested. Bill Gates backed away from a speech. For these power players, the Epstein walls are closing in
